source_concept.label: Explain the relationship between purchasing and supply chain management.

systems_thinking.interaction_description: The relationship between purchasing and supply chain management. and supply chain management have a bidirectional relationship across their domains: decisions in the relationship between purchasing and supply chain management. directly influence how supply chain management is configured and managed, while feedback from supply chain management operations informs adjustments to the relationship between purchasing and supply chain management. strategy.
systems_thinking.explanation: The cross-section relationship between the relationship between purchasing and supply chain management. (Section D) and supply chain management (Section A) illustrates how supply chain concepts rarely exist in isolation. The relationship between purchasing and supply chain management. provides strategic direction that shapes supply chain management decisions, while supply chain management generates operational data and constraints that feed back into the relationship between purchasing and supply chain management. planning. This bidirectional interaction means that changes in either domain can ripple across section boundaries, requiring managers to think holistically rather than in functional silos.

sources.title: Procurement and Supply Chain Management: Key Differences
What is procurement? Definition, Types, Processes & More
SCM Definitions and Glossary of Terms

distractor_descriptions: The relationship between purchasing and supply chain management. and supply chain management operate independently with no meaningful interaction, as they belong to separate functional domains.
The relationship between purchasing and supply chain management. completely subsumes supply chain management, making cross-domain interaction unnecessary since one domain controls the other entirely.

The interaction between the relationship between purchasing and supply chain management. and supply chain management is purely one-directional, with no feedback mechanism from supply chain management back to the relationship between purchasing and supply chain management..

source_concept.label: Explain the relationship between purchasing and supply chain management.

systems_thinking.explanation: The cross-section relationship between the relationship between purchasing and supply chain management. (Section D) and procurement (Section B) illustrates how supply chain concepts rarely exist in isolation. The relationship between purchasing and supply chain management. provides strategic direction that shapes procurement decisions, while procurement generates operational data and constraints that feed back into the relationship between purchasing and supply chain management. planning. This bidirectional interaction means that changes in either domain can ripple across section boundaries, requiring managers to think holistically rather than in functional silos.

This bidirectional interaction means that changes in either domain can ripple across section boundaries, requiring managers to think holistically rather than in functional silos.
